eng: When God began to create the heavens and the earth.
local: Unimbɔti nín kíĺ ki náań kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋee.

eng: the earth was without shape or form, it was dark over the deep sea, and Gods wind swept over the waters.
local: kitiŋ kaa nìn cáá dikíl ní tiba nì tiba kaa nìn bí ki-pu ní dibɔmbɔndi nìn bīī inyiŋfi pu. Kitiŋ nìn sá nnyim ŋma ní, ní Unimbɔti Fam nìn dàn̄ nnyimee pu.

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: Yeesu Kriisitoo naanjab yiŋŋi sɔ̀. U nìn sá Daafiidi naantiiu, ní Daafiidi nìn sá Abraam naantiiu.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Abraam màl̄ Isaaki, ní Isaaki màl̄ Jaakɔb, ní Jaakɔb màl̄ Juuda nì u-nabiyaamu.

eng: God remembered Noah, all those alive, and all the animals with him in the ark. God sent a wind over the earth so that the waters receded.
local: Unimbɔti kaa nìn súntì Noowa nì ipeel nì tiwaŋkuti tin kɔkɔ nìn bí u-cee meela poonee pu. Di sá u nìn yāntì kubuŋu kubɔ dɔ́miń ki jítì kitiŋ pu ní nnyim sīntì.

eng: The LORD said to Noah, Go into the ark with your whole household, because among this generation Ive seen that you are a moral man.
local: Noowa kpáá meela ki dóòyee ní ti-Dindaan bí-u yii: «Á kɔ́ meelan, sii nì a-dicaŋku kɔkɔ, kun puee a-yuntiŋu nib nee kansikinee, sii ní m ká yii a bí deedee.

eng: When the number of people started to increase throughout the fertile land, daughters were born to them.
local: Binib nìn kíĺ ki bíi bùtī kitiŋ pu, ní ki màl̄ mbinimpubiyaamu.

eng: The snake was the most intelligent of all the wild animals that the LORD God had made. He said to the woman, Did God really say that you shouldnt eat from any tree in the garden.
local: Ukoobu di nìn nyí diŋmatindi ipeel in ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti nìn náańee kɔkɔ kansikin. Dalbee ní u dɔ́miń ki bí unimpu yii: «Tiŋman ní Unimbɔti lafun lī yii ní taa ŋmɔ́ bukpaa nee poon subiliŋaaa?».

eng: The man Adam knew his wife Eve intimately. She became pregnant and gave birth to Cain, and said, I have given life to a man with the LORDs help.
local: Adam béè u-nimpuu Awa ní u cáá dipool ki màl̄ Kaayeen, ní ki bí yii: «Ti-Dindaan tútúǹ-mi m màl̄ kibininjabiki.».

eng: God said, Let there be light. And so light appeared.
local: Ní Unimbɔti bí yii: «Ŋwalifi ń nyā!» Ní ŋwalifi nyáń.

eng: The springs of the deep sea and the skies closed up. The skies held back the rain.
local: Anyimbulnimbil nìn ŋéeb́ ní utaa gbēeǹ kaa ti bíi nì.

eng: This is the record of Adams descendants. On the day God created humanity, he made them to resemble God.
local: Adam maaliŋu bɔti sɔ̀. Ŋwiin ŋun Unimbɔti náań unilee, u nìn náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ u-ba ní.

eng: The heavens and the earth and all who live in them were completed.
local: Míǹ ní Unimbɔti náań kutagbɔŋu nì tin kɔkɔ bí ku-puee, ní ki náań kitiŋ nì tin kɔkɔ bí ki-puee ki dóò.

eng: From every clean animal, take seven pairs, a male and his mate; and from every unclean animal, take one pair, a male and his mate.
local: Á kɔ́ǹ tiwaŋkuti tin kɔkɔ ŋān̄ bí dūú ŋá saraayee tiluli tiluli, ijaa nì isal, ní kí kɔ́ǹ tin kaa ŋān̄ bí dūú ŋá saraayee tili tili, uja nì usal.

eng: She gave birth a second time to Cains brother Abel. Abel cared for the flocks, and Cain farmed the fertile land.
local: Míǹ boonee ní u gíti cáá dipool ki màl̄ Kaayeen naal Abeel. Abeel wíiǹee ní ki bíi kpàá tiwaŋkuti, ní u-maan Kaayeen úǹ kù tikpaal.

eng: The waters receded gradually from the earth. After one hundred fifty days, the waters decreased.
local: Ní nnyim kíĺ ki bíi fóotí kitiŋ pu fiii fiii, ki tin cáa sāā iwiin dilaataal nì imuŋku ili nì saalaa.

eng: The divine beings saw how beautiful these human women were, so they married the ones they chose.
local: Unimbɔti jipɔmbi ká yii binifoobi sapɔmbi ŋān̄ee ní bi lēekì bi-kansikin bin fáatiǹ-bee ki cáá.

eng: God saw how good the light was. God separated the light from the darkness.
local: Ní Unimbɔti ká yii ŋwalifi ŋān̄, ní u cúú ŋwalifi nì dibɔmbɔndi ki yākatì tɔbin.

eng: and created them male and female. He blessed them and called them humanity on the day they were created.
local: Uninja nì unimpu ní u nìn náań. U nìn bīiǹ bi-boon ní ki pú-bi diyindi yii unil.

eng: The woman said to the snake, We may eat the fruit of the gardens trees.
local: Ní unimpu kíí-u yii: «Ti ŋūǹ kí ŋmɔ́ isufi in yú bukpaa poonee biliŋ.

eng: On the sixth day God completed all the work that he had done, and on the seventh day God rested from all the work that he had done.
local: Unimbɔti túń u-tuuŋŋi ki dóò, ní ŋwinluluútee, u kāl̀ ki ŋūŋfì atuun ŋin kɔkɔ u ŋée pu.

eng: and from the birds in the sky as well, take seven pairs, male and female, so that their offspring will survive throughout the earth.
local: Á kɔ́ǹ inoon kɔkɔ mun míǹ, ijaa iluli isal iluli, kí ŋmā kí kpāafì i-boolii kitiŋ pu.

eng: Some time later, Cain presented an offering to the LORD from the lands crops.
local: An dāa bāań ŋyunti ŋubɔee ní Kaayeen lēeń u-kpaajinti ki cáań ki ŋá ti-Dindaan saraa.

eng: and in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day, the ark came to rest on the Ararat mountains.
local: Uŋmaluluúti poon iwiin saalku nì ili dal ní meela sīntì ki tɔ́kɔǹ ajoon ŋin bi yíì Araratee pu.

eng: God named the light Day and the darkness Night. There was evening and there was morning: the first day.
local: Ní Unimbɔti yíiń ŋwalifi yii ŋwiimpu ní ki yíiń dibɔmbɔndi yii kunyeeu. Ní kunyeeu mɔ́ ní kutaa dūu wúntì, ŋwimpeepeekaa dal dee.

eng: The LORD said, My breath will not remain in humans forever, because they are flesh. They will live one hundred twenty years.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan bí yii: «Maa làá yàntī kufoou kun m ŋá unilinee ń nīn bí u-ni ki cá jaanjaan. Kun puee u sá tiwun ní ki bí iwɔfin. Waa làá yúntí kí jītí abin dilaataal nì mmuŋku pu.».

eng: God blessed the seventh day and made it holy, because on it God rested from all the work of creation.
local: Míǹ puee Unimbɔti bīiǹ ŋwinluluútee pu ní ki gāntì-ŋu ki síiǹ u-ba, kun puee ŋwiin gbanti ní u kāl̀ ki ŋūŋfì atuun ŋin kɔkɔ u ŋáaǹee pu.

eng: When Adam was years old, he became the father of a son in his image, resembling him, and named him Seth.
local: Adam bàn̄ abin dilaataal nì mmuŋku nì saalaa ní ki màl̄ kibininjabiki ki nántiǹ úǹ u-ba naŋku, ní ki pú-kì diyindi yii Seeti.

eng: but not the fruit of the tree in the middle of the garden. God said, Dont eat from it, and dont touch it, or you will die.
local: Ama busubu bun yú bukpaa kansikinee bil ní Unimbɔti bí yii tí taa ŋmɔ́ kí taa gbēē-bu, an yaa sá fám ti làá kpú.».

eng: In seven days from now I will send rain on the earth for forty days and forty nights. I will wipe off from the fertile land every living thing that I have made.
local: Di sá yii, an kíǹ iwiin iluli dee, m làá yàntī utaa ń nì kitiŋ pu, ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu, iwiin imuŋku ili kí céetí tin m náań tì bí kitiŋ puee kɔkɔ.».

eng: while Abel presented his flocks oldest offspring with their fat. The LORD looked favorably on Abel and his sacrifice.
local: Ní Abeel mun lēeń apibil ŋin u-piii iba péé ki màl̄ee ní ki dū ŋi-nanti tin cáá ŋkpamee ki ŋá ti-Dindaan saraa. Ní ti-Dindaan fōō Abeel nì u-saraau.

eng: The waters decreased gradually until the tenth month, and on the first day of the tenth month the mountain peaks appeared.
local: Nnyim nìn mɔ̀tīi sìntī ki cá ki tin cáa sāā uŋmasaalaati. Uŋmasaalaati gbanti wimpeepeekaaŋu dalee, ní ajoon gbīitìń nnyimin.

eng: God said, Let there be a dome in the middle of the waters to separate the waters from each other.
local: Ní Unimbɔti bí yii: «Kugbɔŋu kubɔ ń nyā kí dàntī kí yàkatī inyiŋfi mili!».

eng: In those days, giants lived on the earth and also afterward, when divine beings and human daughters had sexual relations and gave birth to children. These were the ancient heroes, famous men.
local: Tinikpaandi nìn bí kitiŋ pu ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti jipɔmbi dɔ́miń ki cáá binifoobi sapɔmbi ki màl̄ mbiyaamuee. Míǹ boonee bi nìn gbíntì ki bí. Bíǹ di nìn sá bijanjaliibi bin nìn nyáń diyindi cannaakuee.

eng: After Seths birth, Adam lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Adam nín màl̄ Seeti boonee, u gíti ŋá abin alaataa anii ní. U nìn gíti màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu nì nnimpubiyaamu ki kútì.

eng: This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created. On the day the LORD God made earth and sky.
local: Kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋ náantam nín kíĺ puee dee. Ŋyunti ŋun ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti nyántì kitiŋ nì kutagbɔŋuee.

eng: The snake said to the woman, You wont die!.
local: Niinee ní ukoobu bí unimpu yii: «Kaa sá míǹ, naa làá kpú.

eng: Noah did everything the LORD commanded him.
local: Ní Noowa ŋá kii ti-Dindaan nín bí yii ú ŋá miŋyee.

eng: but didnt look favorably on Cain and his sacrifice. Cain became very angry and looked resentful.
local: ama ní ki fātì u-boon ki tīī Kaayeen nì u-saraau. An puee ní Kaayeen fōō diŋuul tikpil, u-nimbiin cúu bíil̀.

eng: After forty days, Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made.
local: Iwiin imuŋku ili boonee ní Noowa pīitì meela tɔkɔlau un u nìn lēēyee.

eng: God made the dome and separated the waters under the dome from the waters above the dome. And it happened in that way.
local: Ní an ŋá míǹ. Míǹ ní Unimbɔti yāntì kugbɔŋuee nyáń ki dāntì ki cúú inyiŋfi in bí taapuee nì in bí yilpuee ki yākatì.

eng: The LORD saw that humanity had become thoroughly evil on the earth and that every idea their minds thought up was always completely evil.
local: Ti-Dindaan nìn ká yii binib kpitii mɔ̀tīi wīintí kitiŋ pu. Baabadalee tibɔkpitil ŋma di nìn gbí bi-pɔbiliŋ poon bi dàkafī.

eng: In all, Adam lived years, and he died.
local: Abin ŋin kɔkɔ Adam ŋá ní ki nín kpíìyee kpáfì abin alaataa awa nì abin mmuŋku nì saalaa ní.

eng: God knows that on the day you eat from it, you will see clearly and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.
local: Di sá Unimbɔti nyí yii daliŋkin ni yaa ŋmɔ́ busubu gbanti bilee, ni-nimbiliŋ làá ŋúbití ní ní nīn kíǹ ki nyí tiŋan nì tikpitil kii úǹ Unimbɔti nín nyí puee.».

eng: before any wild plants appeared on the earth, and before any field crops grew, because the LORD God hadnt yet sent rain on the earth and there was still no human being to farm the fertile land.
local: kisubiki báà kiba kaa nìn dáá nyáń kitiŋ pu, ní kumoou báà kubɔ mun kaa nìn dáá nyáń, kun puee ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti kaa nìn dáá yāntì utaa nìn̄. Unil mun nìn kpá kí kpɔ̀ tikpaal kitiŋ pu.

eng: Noah was years old when the floodwaters arrived on earth.
local: Noowa nìn cáá abin alaataa aluu ní utaa nìn̄ ní nnyiŋgbeen dɔ́miń kitiŋ pu.

eng: He sent out a raven, and it flew back and forth until the waters over the entire earth had dried up.
local: U nìn dū kulaagaau ki líì taapu, ní ku cá ki gítiń ki cá ki gítiń áli nnyim dāa fóotì ní kitiŋ kūń.

eng: The LORD said to Cain, Why are you angry, and why do you look so resentful?.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan bí Kaayeen yii: «Ba ŋá ní a fōō diŋuul, a-nimbiin cúu bíil̀ mimmee?.

eng: God named the dome Sky. There was evening and there was morning: the second day.
local: Ní Unimbɔti yíiń kugbɔŋuee yii kutagbɔŋu. Ní kunyeeu mɔ́ ní kutaa dūu wúntì, ŋwinliliiti dal dee.

eng: The LORD regretted making human beings on the earth, and he was heartbroken.
local: Ní an wùn̄ ti-Dindaan ki kɔ́-u kunimbaau u nín náań unil kitiŋ puee.

eng: When Seth was years old, he became the father of Enosh.
local: Seeti ŋá abin dilaataal nì abin aŋmoo ní ki màl̄ Eenɔsi.

eng: though a stream rose from the earth and watered all of the fertile land.
local: Ama kitiŋ taapu ní nnyim nìn nyēeń ki sɔ̀ŋkī an kɔkɔ.

eng: The woman saw that the tree was beautiful with delicious food and that the tree would provide wisdom, so she took some of its fruit and ate it, and also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it.
local: Unimpu mun ká yii busubuee bil ŋān̄ ki mɔ̄ diŋmɔl, ki cáá nnimbitaataam, ki lafun máaǹ kí ŋúbití unil nimbiliŋ, ní u kītì ki ŋmɔ́. U-cal bí u-cee ní u tīī u mun ní u ŋmɔ́.

eng: Noah, his sons, his wife, and his sons wives with him entered the ark to escape the floodwaters.
local: Ŋyunti gbantee Noowa nì u-nimpuu nì u-jipɔmbi nì u-coopoobi kɔ́ meelan, nnyiŋgbeen ń taa yòon̄-bi.

eng: Then he sent out a dove to see if the waters on all of the fertile land had subsided.
local: U nìn tí dū dibeenanjil ki líì kí kpèè yii nnyim yaa bātì kitiŋ pu.

eng: If you do the right thing, wont you be accepted? But if you dont do the right thing, sin will be waiting at the door ready to strike! It will entice you, but you must rule over it.
local: A yaa bàà ŋá tin ŋān̄ee, a-yil bàà gáań ní. A yaa kaa ŋá tin ŋān̄ee, ikpiti kpátì ki dɔ̄ a-punyɔkɔpu ki nyàab̀-si, ama sii pɔ̄ɔkiǹ-i.».

eng: God said, Let the waters under the sky come together into one place so that the dry land can appear. And thats what happened.
local: Ní Unimbɔti bí yii: «Nnyim min dɔ̄ taapuee ń kóoǹ kí kpāfì dijandi diba, dijandi din kíǹee ń nīn sá ŋkulimmi!» Ní an ŋá míǹ.

eng: So the LORD said, I will wipe off of the land the human race that Ive created: from human beings to livestock to the crawling things to the birds in the skies, because I regret I ever made them.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan bí yii: «See m kúntì binib bin m náańee kitiŋ pu. M làá kúntí-bi kí kpáaǹ tiwaŋkuti nì tin kɔkɔ fù ti-biŋŋi puee nì inoon kɔkɔ, kun puee an wùn̄-mi m nín náań-bee pu.».

eng: After the birth of Enosh, Seth lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Seeti nín màl̄ Eenɔsi boonee, u gíti ŋá abin alaataa anii nì abin aluli ní. U nìn gíti màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu nì nnimpubiyaamu ki kútì.

eng: the LORD God formed the human from the topsoil of the fertile land and blew lifes breath into his nostrils. The human came to life.
local: Ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti dū ntam ní ki máń unil, ní ki púfì ŋfam ki ŋá u-maŋfataabuŋyin, ní u kpántì unifoo.

eng: Then they both saw clearly and knew that they were naked. So they sewed fig leaves together and made garments for themselves.
local: Niinee ní bi-liitil nimbiliŋ cúu ŋúbitì ní bi ká yii bi bí dicancaŋgbeel, ní bi tīfì busubu bun bi yíì fiigee faati ki ŋáĺ ki dūu lōoǹ.

eng: From the clean and unclean animals, from the birds and everything crawling on the ground.
local: Tiwaŋkuti tin ŋān̄ bí dūú ŋá saraayee nì tin kaa ŋān̄ bí dūú ŋá saraayee, nì inoon nì tin kɔkɔ pée fù kitiŋ puee.

eng: Cain said to his brother Abel, Lets go out to the field. When they were in the field, Cain attacked his brother Abel and killed him.
local: Niinee ní Kaayeen sòon̄ nì u-naal Abeel. Ŋyunti ŋun bi bí kusaaunee ní Kaayeen cúú u-naal Abeel ki kūū.

eng: but the dove found no place to set its foot. It returned to him in the ark since waters still covered the entire earth. Noah stretched out his hand, took it, and brought it back into the ark.
local: Ama dibeenanjil kaa ká tin pu di làá tɔ́kɔńee ní ki fātìi nín gítí u-cee meela poon, kun puee nnyim nìn dáá gbíntìi bí kitiŋ kɔkɔ pu. Ní Noowa tāntì u-ŋal ki cúú-dì ki kɔ́ǹ u-cee meela poon.

eng: God named the dry land Earth, and he named the gathered waters Seas. God saw how good it was.
local: Ní u yíiń dijandi din kūńee yii kitiŋ, ní ki yíiń nnyim min kóoǹ ki kpáfèe yii tiŋku. Ní u ká yii míǹ ŋān̄.

eng: But as for Noah, the LORD approved of him.
local: Ama Noowa baba di nìn fáatì ti-Dindaan.

eng: In all, Seth lived years, and he died.
local: Abin ŋin kɔkɔ Seeti ŋá ní ki nín kpíìyee kpáfì abin alaataa awa nì abin saalaa nì ali ní.

eng: During that days cool evening breeze, they heard the sound of the LORD God walking in the garden; and the man and his wife hid themselves from the LORD God in the middle of the gardens trees.
local: Kujoou jóotì kubuŋu bíi jītí fílfilee ní bi gbìl̄ ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti pu u bí ki còom̄ bukpaan. Ní bi sáǹ ki bál̀-u bukpaayee sufii kansikin.

eng: two of each, male and female, went into the ark with Noah, just as God commanded Noah.
local: kubuluu kamaa uja nì usal nìn dɔ́miń ki kɔ́ meela poon Noowa cee kii Unimbɔti nín nìn pée tùkū-u puee.

eng: The LORD God planted a garden in Eden in the east and put there the human he had formed.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti náań isufi kpaabu Eedeen tiŋki poon, bu bí ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú, ní ki dū unil un u máńee ki kāaǹ bukpaayee poon.

eng: The LORD said to Cain, Where is your brother Abel? Cain said, I dont know. Am I my brothers guardian.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan bālifì Kaayeen yii: «A-naal Abeel bí la?» Ní u kíí-u yii: «Maa nyí. Man di cí m-naalaaa?».

eng: He waited seven more days and sent the dove out from the ark again.
local: Ní u gíti kāl̀ iwiin iluli boonee ní ki tí nyántì dinanjil ki líì.

eng: God said, Let the earth grow plant life: plants yielding seeds and fruit trees bearing fruit with seeds inside it, each according to its kind throughout the earth. And thats what happened.
local: Unimbɔti bí yii: «Kumɔsɔŋu ń nyā kitiŋ pu, kumoou kamaa ń lòoǹ ku-boolii ní isufi mun ń nyā, busubu kamaa ń lòoǹ bu-boolii!» Ní an ŋá míǹ.

eng: When Enosh was years old, he became the father of Kenan.
local: Eenɔsi ŋá abin imuŋku inaa nì saalaa ní ki màl̄ Keenan.

eng: These are Noahs descendants. In his generation, Noah was a moral and exemplary man; he walked with God.
local: Noowa nì u-biyaamu bɔti sɔ̀. Noowa nìn sá unimɔnti ní ki bí deedee u-yuntiŋu nib kansikin. U nìn nóó Unimbɔti mmɔntiim.

eng: The LORD God called to the man and said to him, Where are you.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti yíiń uninja yii: «A bí la ní?».

eng: After seven days, the floodwaters arrived on the earth.
local: Iwiin iluli boonee, ní utaa nìn̄, ní nnyiŋgbeen dɔ́miń ki bīiǹ kitiŋ pu.

eng: In the fertile land, the LORD God grew every beautiful tree with edible fruit, and also he grew the tree of life in the middle of the garden and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
local: Ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti yāntì isufi in biliŋ ŋān̄ ŋkpèé nì diŋmɔlee nyáń ki bí i-ba i-ba, ní busubu bun tìì dimaŋfalee nì bun yàntī bi béetí tiŋan nì tikpitilee yú bukpaayee kansikin.

eng: The earth produced plant life: plants yielding seeds, each according to its kind, and trees bearing fruit with seeds inside it, each according to its kind. God saw how good it was.
local: Ní kumɔsɔŋu nyáń kitiŋ pu, ní timool tin làá lòoǹ ibee lòon̄ tì nín sá dimɔcil diŋyee boolii, ní busubu kamaa mun lòon̄ bu nín sá disucil diŋyee boolii. Ní Unimbɔti ká yii míǹ ŋān̄.

eng: The dove came back to him in the evening, grasping a torn olive leaf in its beak. Then Noah knew that the waters were subsiding from the earth.
local: Kujoouee ní dibeenanjil fātìi gítì u-cee ki bàb̄ ɔliifi faasɔŋku. Niinee ní Noowa béè yii nnyim fóotì ki bātì kitiŋ pu.

eng: The LORD said, What did you do? The voice of your brothers blood is crying to me from the ground.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan bí-u yii: «Ba ní a ŋée! A-naal fatikuu di gbáàń ibiil taapu ki dāa bāań man nee.

eng: After Kenans birth, Enosh lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eenɔsi nín màl̄ Keenan boonee, u gíti ŋá abin alaataa anii nì abin saalku ní. U nìn gíti màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu nì nnimpubiyaamu kí kútì.

eng: Noah had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
local: U nìn màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu muta. Bi-yiŋŋi di sá Seem nì Kaam nì Jaafeeti.

eng: The man replied, I heard your sound in the garden; I was afraid because I was naked, and I hid myself.
local: Ní u kíí-u yii: «M nín bí bukpaan ki gbìl̄ a-puee ní m fāŋkì, kun puee m bí dicancaŋgbeel, an pu ní m bál̀.».

eng: In the six hundredth year of Noahs life, in the second month, on the seventeenth day—on that day all the springs of the deep sea erupted, and the windows in the skies opened.
local: Noowa biŋŋi alaataa aluu poon, dibindi ŋmaliliitiu iwiin saalku nì ili dal ní inyimbulkpaan cúu pīitì, ní nnyim cílìń, ní yilpu cúu cōotì ní nnyim sìtīń.

eng: A river flows from Eden to water the garden, and from there it divides into four headwaters.
local: Ní mbun ŋubɔ nyēeń Eedeen tiŋkin ki sɔ̀ŋkī bukpaayee. Bukpaayee poon ní mbunee cúu yākatì abuntaa anaa.

eng: There was evening and there was morning: the third day.
local: Ní kunyeeu mɔ́ ní kutaa dūu wúntì, ŋwintataati dal dee.

eng: You are now cursed from the ground that opened its mouth to take your brothers blood from your hand.
local: Kɔŋkɔnnee a nín kūū a-naal kitiŋ nyùn̄ u-fatikuuee, m tɔ́-si mbusu.

eng: In Gods sight, the earth had become corrupt and was filled with violence.
local: Kitiŋ nìn bíil̀ Unimbɔti nimbiin. Tipɔmbɔti ní binib nìn kíǹ ki dàkà tikɔkɔ poon.

eng: He waited seven more days and sent out the dove, but it didnt come back to him again.
local: Ní u gíti tí kāl̀ iwiin iluli, boonee ní u fātìi tí nyántì dibeenanjil ki líì ní dàa ti gítì u-cee.

eng: In all, Enosh lived years, and he died.
local: Abin ŋin kɔkɔ Eenɔsi ŋá ní ki nín kpíìyee kpáfì abin alaataa awa nì abin aŋmoo ní.

eng: It rained on the earth forty days and forty nights.
local: Utaa nìn nìn̄ iwiin imuŋku ili, ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu.

eng: He said, Who told you that you were naked? Did you eat from the tree, which I commanded you not to eat.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti bí-u yii: «Ŋma tùkū-si yii a bí dicancaŋgbeel? Àá a ŋmɔ́ busubu bun bil m kpáafì-si yii á taa ŋmɔ́ee ní?».

eng: The name of the first river is the Pishon. It flows around the entire land of Havilah, where there is gold.
local: Dibuntaapeekaal ní bi yíì Piisɔn. Díǹ di māntìi tú Afiila tiŋki kin poon ní sindaariya bée.

eng: God said, Let there be lights in the dome of the sky to separate the day from the night. They will mark events, sacred seasons, days, and years.
local: Ní Unimbɔti bí yii: «Iwalfi ń nyā kí nīn bí kutagbɔŋu pu kí yàkatī ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu, bí nīn cáa dāantí iyunti, kí nīn cáa kàaǹ iwiin nì abin nì iwiŋkpaan.

eng: When you farm the fertile land, it will no longer grow anything for you, and you will become a roving nomad on the earth.
local: A yaa ti kùn̄ee, kitiŋ kaa làá ti lòoǹ. A làá nīn kíǹ ki tú ki yìiǹ an kɔkɔ ní kitiŋ pu.».

eng: God saw that the earth was corrupt, because all creatures behaved corruptly on the earth.
local: Unimbɔti nìn ká yii ikpiti jíń kitiŋ, di sá yii binib nìn dū bi-ba ki líì nì tikpitil.

eng: When Kenan was years old, he became the father of Mahalalel.
local: Keenan ŋá abin imuŋku ita nì abin saalaa ní ki màl̄ Maalaleeyeel.

eng: In Noahs six hundred first year, on the first day of the first month, the waters dried up from the earth. Noah removed the arks hatch and saw that the surface of the fertile land had dried up.
local: Noowa biŋŋi alaataa aluu nì dibindi diba, uŋmapeepeekaa wimpeekaaŋu dalee, ní u pīitì meela yilpu ki kpēeń taapuee ní ki ká yii nnyim fóotì kitiŋ pu.

eng: That same day Noah, with his sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, Noahs wife, and his sons three wives, went into the ark.
local: An wiiŋŋu ní Noowa nì u-nimpuu nì u-jipɔmbi Seem nì Kaam nì Jaafeeti nì bi-nimpoobi bitee kɔ́ meelan.

eng: That lands gold is pure, and the land also has sweet-smelling resins and gemstones.
local: Kitiŋ gbanti sindaariyau sá umɔnti ní, ní talaalu un bi yíì bideeliyoomee nì ataŋkpamɔnti ŋin bi yíì soowamee bí niin.

eng: The man said, The woman you gave me, she gave me some fruit from the tree, and I ate.
local: Ní uninja kíí-u yii: «Unimpu un a cáań ki ŋàl̄-mee di kītì disubil gbanti ki tīī-mi ní m ŋmɔ́.».

eng: They will be lights in the dome of the sky to shine on the earth. And thats what happened.
local: iwalfi gbantee ń nīn bí kutagbɔŋu pu ki ŋmíntí kitiŋ pu.» Ní an ŋá míǹ.

eng: God said to Noah, The end has come for all creatures, since they have filled the earth with violence. I am now about to destroy them along with the earth.
local: Ní Unimbɔti bí Noowa yii: «M làá kúntí binib kɔkɔ, kun puee bi yāntì tipɔmbɔti jíń kitiŋ kɔkɔ. M làá céetí-bi kitiŋ pu.

eng: Cain said to the LORD, My punishment is more than I can bear.
local: Ní Kaayeen bí ti-Dindaan yii: «Óo, m-yìntitam gìńdi nyìmiǹ-mi maa ŋūǹ kí túń.

eng: After the birth of Mahalalel, Kenan lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Keenan nín màl̄ Maalaleeyeel boonee, u gíti ŋá abin alaataa anii nì abin imuŋku ili ní. U nìn gíti màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu nì nnimpubiyaamu ki kútì.

eng: In the second month, on the twenty-seventh day, the earth was dry.
local: Uŋmaliliiti iwiin mmuŋku nì iluli dalee ní kitiŋ kūń.

eng: They and every kind of animal—every kind of livestock, every kind that crawls on the ground, every kind of bird.
local: Ŋwiin gbanti ní upeel kamaa buluku, kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku nì tin kɔkɔ fù kitiŋ puee kubuluu kubuluu, nì unoon kamaa buluku, nì tin kɔkɔ cáá ifeenee kubuluu kubuluu mun kɔ́ meelan.

eng: The name of the second river is the Gihon. It flows around the entire land of Cush.
local: Dibuntaaliliitiilee ní bi yíì Giiyɔn. Díǹ di māntìi tú Kusa tiŋki kɔkɔ.

eng: God made the stars and two great lights: the larger light to rule over the day and the smaller light to rule over the night.
local: Míǹ ní Unimbɔti ŋá iwalfikpaan ili: ŋkpaŋkpaan di sá ŋwìiń, kutaa yaa wúntèe, ŋú nīn bí ki ŋmíntí. Kiwaai di sá uŋmal, kunyeeuee, ú nīn bí ki ŋmíntí, ní ki ŋá iŋmalbijaa mun.

eng: so make a wooden ark. Make the ark with nesting places and cover it inside and out with tar.
local: Á dū busubu bun bi yíì goofeeree dɔi kí kpáá meela, kí pìtī poon adiin kí bɔ̀ntī poon nì boon aŋɔ.

eng: The LORD God said to the woman, What have you done?! And the woman said, The snake tricked me, and I ate.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti bí unimpu yii: «Ba di yì-tì ní a ŋá míǹ?» Ní unimpu kíí-u yii: «Ukoobu di kíil̀-mi ní m ŋmɔ́.».

eng: In all, Kenan lived years, and he died.
local: Abin ŋin kɔkɔ Keenan ŋá ki nín kpíìyee kpáfì abin alaataa awa nì abin saalaa ní.

eng: God spoke to Noah.
local: Niinee ní Unimbɔti bí Noowa yii:.

eng: Now that youve driven me away from the fertile land and I am hidden from your presence, Im about to become a roving nomad on the earth, and anyone who finds me will kill me.
local: A yaa jìn̄-mi díǹ nee kitiŋ pu an sá yii ḿ nīn cá ndandati kí taa nín̄ pìlīǹ-si, kí nīn tú ki yìiǹee, ubɔ yaa ká-mee, see ú kpɔ̀-mi.».

eng: they came to Noah and entered the ark, two of every creature that breathes.
local: Tiwan tin kɔkɔ pée cáá kufoouee nóoń tɔb tili tili, ki kɔ́ Noowa cee, meela poon.

eng: The name of the third river is the Tigris, flowing east of Assyria; and the name of the fourth river is the Euphrates.
local: Bi nìn yíì dibuntaatataatiilee yii Tiiga. Díǹ di pùubìń Asuur tiŋkin, ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú. Dibuntaananaatiil ní bi yíì Eefiraati.

eng: The LORD God said to the snake, Because you did this, you are the one cursed out of all the farm animals, out of all the wild animals. On your belly you will crawl, and dust you will eat every day of your life.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti bí ukoobu yii: «An nín ŋá pu ní a ŋá mimmee, m tɔ́-si mbusu tiwaŋkuti nì ipeel kɔkɔ kansikin. A làá nīn kíǹ ki fù a-bindi pu ní. A-jinti di làá nīn kíǹ ki sá ŋkpɔŋkpɔm kí tin sāā a-kúm dal.

eng: God put them in the dome of the sky to shine on the earth.
local: Ní Unimbɔti dū i-kɔkɔ ki kāaǹ kutagbɔŋu pu í nīn ŋmíntí kitiŋ pu.

eng: When Mahalalel was years old, he became the father of Jared.
local: Maalaleeyeel ŋá abin imuŋku ita nì abin aŋmoo ní ki màl̄ Jeereedi.

eng: Go out of the ark, you and your wife, your sons, and your sons wives with you.
local: «Nyā meelan, sii nì a-nimpuu nì a-jipɔmbi nì a-coopoobi.

eng: This is how you should make it: four hundred fifty feet long, seventy-five feet wide, and forty-five feet high.
local: Á kpáá-bu mpúun ń nīn cáá meetab dilaataal nì imuŋku ili nì saalaa, ŋwaan ń nīn cáá meetab mmuŋku nì biŋmoo, ŋfɔkɔn ń nīn sá meetab saalku.

eng: The LORD said to him, It wont happen; anyone who kills Cain will be paid back seven times. The LORD put a sign on Cain so that no one who found him would assault him.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan bí-u yii: «Aayee! Un di yaa kūū Kaayeeŋyee, bi làá téeǹ udaan min min u ŋée miluli pu ní.» Ní ti-Dindaan ŋá u-pu kudaaŋŋu kubɔ yii ubɔ yaa ká-uee, ú taa kpɔ̀-u.

eng: Male and female of every creature went in, just as God had commanded him. Then the LORD closed the door behind them.
local: Tin kɔkɔ pée cáá kufoouee, uja nì usal nìn kɔ́ meelan kii Unimbɔti nín nìn pée tùkū Noowa puee. Ní ti-Dindaan dūu pīiǹ u-pu.

eng: The LORD God took the human and settled him in the garden of Eden to farm it and to take care of it.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti dū unil ki kāaǹ Eedeen kpaabun ú nīn kù-bu kí nīn kpèé-bu.

eng: I will put contempt between you and the woman, between your offspring and hers. They will strike your head, but you will strike at their heels.
local: M làá náań kudɔ̀ŋu kí bíĺ sii nì unimpu kansikin, kí bíĺ a-maaliŋu nì u-maaliŋu kansikin. U-maaliŋu làá dāá tāā kí mátí a-yil, ní sii ń dāá jú úǹ tagbíndi.».

eng: After Jareds birth, Mahalalel lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Maalaleeyeel nín màl̄ Jeereedi boonee, u gíti ŋá abin alaataa anii nì abin mmuŋku nì saalaa ní. U nìn gíti màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu nì nnimpubiyaamu ki kútì.

eng: to rule over the day and over the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. God saw how good it was.
local: ŋubɔ ń nīn bí ki ŋmíntí ŋwiimpu, ŋun kíǹee ń nīn bí ki ŋmíntí kunyeeu. Unimbɔti ká yii míǹ ŋān̄.

eng: Bring out with you all the animals of every kind—birds, livestock, everything crawling on the ground—so that they may populate the earth, be fertile, and multiply on the earth.
local: Nyāntí tin kɔkɔ pée cáá kufoou ki bí a-ceeyee, an dàkà yii inoon nì tiwaŋkuti nì tin kɔkɔ fù kitiŋee mun, tí kpátí kí gbí kitiŋ pu kí màal̀ kí bùtī kí gbí kitiŋ.».

eng: Cain left the LORDs presence, and he settled down in the land of Nod, east of Eden.
local: Ní Kaayeen búntì ndandati kaa ti pìl̄ ti-Dindaan, ki tin kóòń kitiŋ kin bi yíì Noodee. A yaa bí Eedeenee, kì bí ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú ní.

eng: Make a roof for the ark and complete it one foot from the top. Put a door in its side. In the hold below, make the second and third decks.
local: A yaa ŋée, á kpáá kí bìin̄ yilpu, ní kí yàntī mbùń dimeetajandi ń nīn bí a nín kpáá ki bīiǹ puee nì meela guŋŋi kansikin, ní kí gíí kipunyɔkɔ dijandi diba pú, ní kí pìtī meela poon aditɔntɔkɔ ata.

eng: The flood remained on the earth for forty days. The waters rose, lifted the ark, and it rode high above the earth.
local: Ní utaa nìn̄ iwiin imuŋku ili. Ní nnyim gbí kitiŋ pu ní meela yíkì ki dēeǹ nnyim pu.

eng: The LORD God commanded the human, Eat your fill from all of the gardens trees.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti síiǹ unil mara nee yii: «A ŋūǹ kí ŋmɔ́ isufi in yú bukpaanee kɔkɔ biliŋ.

eng: In all, Mahalalel lived years, and he died.
local: Abin ŋin kɔkɔ Maalaleeyeel ŋá ki nín kpíìyee kpáfì abin aŋmoo kpá alaataa awa ní.

eng: To the woman he said, I will make your pregnancy very painful; in pain you will bear children. You will desire your husband, but he will rule over you.
local: Ní u bí unimpu yii: «A nín ŋá miŋyee, m làá gbéeǹ-si timatíǹdi gìńdi. Nì falaa ní a làá nīn màal̀ a-biyaamu. A-cal neeliŋu làá nīn pée cáá-si, ama úǹ di làá nīn cáá sii pu mpɔn.».

eng: There was evening and there was morning: the fourth day.
local: Ní kunyeeu mɔ́ ní kutaa dūu wúntì, ŋwinnanaati dal dee.

eng: When Jared was years old, he became the father of Enoch.
local: Jeereedi ŋá abin dilaataal nì imuŋku ita nì abin ali ní ki màl̄ Eenɔki.

eng: but dont eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, because on the day you eat from it, you will die.
local: ama busubu bun bil a yaa ŋmɔ́ a làá béé tiŋan nì tikpitilee ní á taa ŋmɔ́. Daliŋkin a yaa ŋmɔ́ busubu gbanti bilee, a làá kpú ní.».

eng: To the man he said, Because you listened to your wifes voice and you ate from the tree that I commanded, Dont eat from it, cursed is the fertile land because of you; in pain you will eat from it every day of your life.
local: Ní ki bí uninja yii: «A nín ŋá pu ki pílifì a-nimpuu nyɔkɔ ní ki ŋmɔ́ busubu bun bil man kpáafì-si yii á taa ŋmɔ́ee, a-puee m tɔ́ kitiŋ mbusu. A làá nīn ŋáań falaa ní kí nín ká tijin kí jī. Míǹ ní án làá nīn bí-si áli kí tin sāā a-kúm dal.

eng: God said, Let the waters swarm with living things, and let birds fly above the earth up in the dome of the sky.
local: Ní Unimbɔti bí yii: «Tiwan tin cáá kufoouee ń kpátí kí gbí nnyim poon dàaa, inoon mun ń nīn bí kutaa pu ki ŋàaǹ.».

eng: After Enochs birth, Jared lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Jeereedi nín màl̄ Eenɔki boonee, u gíti ŋá abin alaataa anii ní. U nìn gíti màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu nì nnimpubiyaamu ki kútì.

eng: Then the LORD God said, Its not good that the human is alone. I will make him a helper that is perfect for him.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti bí yii: «An kaa ŋān̄ unil ń nīn bí u-baba míǹ. M làá náań un làá nīn tútúǹ-uee kí ŋàl̄-u.».

eng: God created the great sea animals and all the tiny living things that swarm in the waters, each according to its kind, and all the winged birds, each according to its kind. God saw how good it was.
local: Ní Unimbɔti náań tiwambilindi tin kóò nnyiminee nì tin kɔkɔ cáá kufoou ki kpátìi gbí nnyim poonee nì ti-buluku ti-buluku. U náań inoon kɔkɔ nì i-buluku i-buluku. Unimbɔti ká yii míǹ ŋān̄.

eng: Weeds and thistles will grow for you, even as you eat the fields plants.
local: «Apɔn nì abɔjanaakun di làá nīn nyēē a-kpaabun, a-kpaajinti ní a làá nīn jīǹ.

eng: In all, Jared lived years, and he died.
local: Abin ŋin kɔkɔ Jeereedi ŋá ki nín kpíìyee kpáfì abin alaataa awa nì imuŋku ita nì abin ali ní.

eng: So the LORD God formed from the fertile land all the wild animals and all the birds in the sky and brought them to the human to see what he would name them. The human gave each living being its name.
local: Ní u dū ntam ki máń ipeel nì inoon kɔkɔ ní ki cáań-i unil cee kí kpèè u nín làá pɔ̄-i ayin puee, án nín làá ŋá pu ní ipeel kɔkɔ ń nīn cáá ayin ŋin unil pú-iee.

eng: Then God blessed them: Be fertile and multiply and fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth.
local: Ní Unimbɔti bīiǹ ti-boon ki bí-tì yii: «Ní bùtī kí gbí tiŋku, inoon mun ń bùtī kí gbí kitiŋ!».

eng: by the sweat of your face you will eat bread— until you return to the fertile land, since from it you were taken; you are soil, to the soil you will return.
local: See á tɔ̄ ditundi kí káaĺ kutuntuŋu a-yigbeel pu ní kí wàatī kí nín ká tijin kí jī. Án làá nīn pée bí-si míǹ ní, áli á dāá fàtīí gítí ntam min m lēē ki máń-see poon. Kun puee a sá ŋkpɔŋkpɔm ní ki làá fàtīí gítí ŋkpɔŋkpɔm.».

eng: When Enoch was years old, he became the father of Methuselah.
local: Eenɔki ŋá abin imuŋku ita nì abin aŋmoo ní ki màl̄ Matusaleem.

eng: The human named all the livestock, all the birds in the sky, and all the wild animals. But a helper perfect for him was nowhere to be found.
local: Míǹ ní unil pú tiwaŋkuti kɔkɔ nì inoon nì ipeel kɔkɔ ayin. Ama unil u-ba kaa ká un làá nīn bí ki kɔ́ĺ-u kí nīn tútúǹ-uee.

eng: There was evening and there was morning: the fifth day.
local: Ní kunyeeu mɔ́ ní kutaa dūu wúntì, ŋwiŋŋmooŋmooti dal dee.

eng: The man named his wife Eve because she is the mother of everyone who lives.
local: Ní uninja pú u-nimpuu diyindi yii Awa, taapu di sá yii un bí dimaŋfalinee, kun puee úǹ di sá binifoobi kɔkɔ na.

eng: Enoch walked with God. After Methuselahs birth, Enoch lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eenɔki nín màl̄ Matusaleemee, u nóó Unimbɔti deedee áli abin alaataa ata. U nìn gíti màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu nì nnimpubiyaamu ki kútì.

eng: So the LORD God put the human into a deep and heavy sleep, and took one of his ribs and closed up the flesh over it.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti yāntì ŋgeembiim miba dɔ́miń ki cúú unil ní u gèen̄. Ŋyunti ŋun u bíi gèeǹee ní ti-Dindaan lēē u-takakalkpabil diba ní ki fātìi dūu bīiǹ.

eng: God said, Let the earth produce every kind of living thing: livestock, crawling things, and wildlife. And thats what happened.
local: Ní Unimbɔti bí yii: «Tiwan tin fòotēe kɔkɔ kubuluu kubuluu ń nyā kí nīn bí kitiŋ pu, an dàkà yii tiwaŋkuti nì tin fù ti-biŋŋi puee nì ipeel ń nyā!» Ní an ŋá míǹ.

eng: The LORD God made the man and his wife leather clothes and dressed them.
local: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti dū tigban ki lōoǹ Adam nì u-nimpuu.

eng: In all, Enoch lived years.
local: Abin ŋin kɔkɔ Eenɔki kāl̀ kitiŋ puee kpáfì abin alaataa ata nì imuŋku ita nì abin aŋmoo ní.

eng: With the rib taken from the human, the LORD God fashioned a woman and brought her to the human being.
local: Ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti dū ditakakalkpabil din u lēē unilinee ki máń unimpu ní ki cáań-u unil cee.

eng: God made every kind of wildlife, every kind of livestock, and every kind of creature that crawls on the ground. God saw how good it was.
local: Ní Unimbɔti nyántì kumooun peelii kɔkɔ kubuluu kubuluu nì tiwaŋkuti kɔkɔ kubuluu kubuluu nì tin kɔkɔ fù ti-biŋŋi puee kubuluu kubuluu. Ní Unimbɔti ká yii míǹ ŋān̄.

eng: The LORD God said, The human being has now become like one of us, knowing good and evil. Now, so he doesnt stretch out his hand and take also from the tree of life and eat and live forever.
local: U ŋá míǹ ki dóòyee ní ki bí yii: «Kɔŋkɔnnee, unil kpántì kii ti-kansikin ubɔ dee, ki kíǹ ki nyí tiŋan nì tikpitil. Tí taa yàntī ú ŋmɔ́ dimaŋfal subil kí nīn bí jaanjaan kaa kúù.».

eng: Enoch walked with God and disappeared because God took him.
local: Eenɔki nìn nóó Unimbɔti deedee, an puee Unimbɔti yōoǹ-u baa ti ká-u.

eng: The human said, This one finally is bone from my bones and flesh from my flesh. She will be called a woman because from a man she was taken.
local: Ní unil bí yii: «Kpèè min bí nee, m-kpabiŋ kpabil sèé, m-wunnanti nanti sèé. Un bí nee ní bi làá nīn yíì unimpu. Kun puee uninjan ní bi lēeń-u.».

eng: Then God said, Let us make humanity in our image to resemble us so that they may take charge of the fish of the sea, the birds in the sky, the livestock, all the earth, and all the crawling things on earth.
local: Ní Unimbɔti bí yii: «Tí náań unil kí nántíǹ ti-ba, ú nīn náań nì ti, kí nīn cáá mpɔn ijɔm nì inoon pu, kí nīn cáá mpɔn tiwaŋkuti nì ipeel kɔkɔ nì tin kɔkɔ fù ti-biŋŋi pu ki bí kitiŋ puee pu!».

eng: When Methuselah was years old, he became the father of Lamech.
local: Matusaleem ŋá abin dilaataal nì imuŋku inaa nì abin aluli ní ki màl̄ Leemeeki.

eng: the LORD God sent him out of the garden of Eden to farm the fertile land from which he was taken.
local: Niinee ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti jìn̄-u Eedeen kpaabun ú cù kí nīn kù kitiŋ kin pu bi lēē ki máń-uee.

eng: This is the reason that a man leaves his father and mother and embraces his wife, and they become one flesh.
local: An pu ní uninja dāa nyēē u-ba kaa ti bí u-baa nì u-na cee ní u nì u-nimpuu ń kɔ́ tɔbin, ní kí kpántí ŋwuŋgbambaanti.

eng: God created humanity in Gods own image, in the divine image God created them, male and female God created them.
local: Ní Unimbɔti náań unil ki nántiǹ u-ba, u náań-u ki nántiǹ úǹ Unimbɔti u-ba ní. U nìn náań uninja nì unimpu ní.

eng: After Lamechs birth, Methuselah lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Matusaleem nín màl̄ Leemeeki boonee, u gíti ŋá abin alaataa aluli nì imuŋku inaa nì abin ali ní. U nìn gíti màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu nì nnimpubiyaamu ki kútì.

eng: He drove out the human. To the east of the garden of Eden, he stationed winged creatures wielding flaming swords to guard the way to the tree of life.
local: U jìn̄ unil mimmee ní ki cáań keerubimbiki yóoǹ bukpaayee nyɔkɔpu ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú, bi ŋúb́ kitàataajiki ki fīintí an ŋmóò làka laka, ki yú dimaŋfal subu saŋŋu pu ki cí-ŋu.

eng: The two of them were naked, the man and his wife, but they werent embarrassed.
local: Uninja nì unimpu nìn pée bí dicancaŋgbeel ní kaa nìn cáá tɔb pu icoo.

eng: God blessed them and said to them, Be fertile and multiply; fill the earth and master it. Take charge of the fish of the sea, the birds in the sky, and everything crawling on the ground.
local: Ní Unimbɔti bīiǹ bi-boon ki bí-bi yii: «Ní màal̀ kí bùtī, binib ń gbí kitiŋ. Ní nīn cáá mpɔn kitiŋ pu nì ijɔm nì inoon nì tin kɔkɔ pée fù ti-biŋŋi pu ki bí kitiŋ puee pu!».

eng: In all, Methuselah lived years, and he died.
local: Abin ŋin kɔkɔ Matusaleem ŋá ki nín kpíìyee kpáfì abin alaataa awa nì imuŋku ita nì abin awa.

eng: Then God said, I now give to you all the plants on the earth that yield seeds and all the trees whose fruit produces its seeds within it. These will be your food.
local: Ní Unimbɔti kútì yii: «M dū tin kɔkɔ lòoǹ ibi ki yú kitiŋ jaŋŋi kɔkɔ puee nì isufi in lòoǹ asubilee ki tīī-ni, án nīn sá ni-jinti.

eng: Cain knew his wife intimately. She became pregnant and gave birth to Enoch. Cain built a city and named the city after his son Enoch.
local: Kaayeen cáá unimpu, ní u cáá dipool ki màl̄ Eenɔki. Boonee ní u máń adiin ní an dūu kpántì kitiŋ, ní u dū u-jipɔɔn yindi Eenɔki ki pú-kì.

eng: So Noah went out of the ark with his sons, his wife, and his sons wives.
local: Ní Noowa nyáń meela poon u nì u-nimpuu nì u-jipɔmbi nì u-coopoobi.

eng: I am now bringing the floodwaters over the earth to destroy everything under the sky that breathes. Everything on earth is about to take its last breath.
local: Man làá yàntī utaa ń nì, ní nnyiŋgbeen ń kúntí tin kɔkɔ pée cáá kufoou ki bí kitiŋ puee, tin kɔkɔ bí kitiŋ puee làá céetí.

eng: The waters rose and spread out over the earth. The ark floated on the surface of the waters.
local: Ní nnyim gbí ki bīiǹ kitiŋ kɔkɔ, ní meela kíǹ ki dēē nnyim pu.

eng: When Lamech was years old, he became the father of a son.
local: Leemeeki ŋá abin dilaataal nì imuŋku inaa nì abin ali ní ki màl̄ kibininjabiki.

eng: To all wildlife, to all the birds in the sky, and to everything crawling on the ground—to everything that breathes—I give all the green grasses for food. And thats what happened.
local: Míǹ mbaantiim, m dū timool ki tīī tiwaŋkuti tin bí kitiŋ puee kɔkɔ nì inoon kɔkɔ nì tin kɔkɔ pée fù ti-biŋŋi pu, ki bí kitiŋ pu ki cáá kufoouee án nīn sá tì-jinti.» Ní an ŋá míǹ.

eng: Irad was born to Enoch. Irad fathered Mehujael, Mehujael fathered Methushael, and Methushael fathered Lamech.
local: Ní Eenɔki màl̄ Yiraadi, ní Yiraadi màl̄ Meewujayeel, ní Meewujayeel màl̄ Meetukayeel, ní Meetukayeel màl̄ Leemeeki.

eng: But I will set up my covenant with you. You will go into the ark together with your sons, your wife, and your sons wives.
local: Ama m làá pòon̄ sii dipoondi. A làá dāá kɔ́ meelan, sii nì a-nimpuu nì a-jipɔmbi nì a-coopoobi.

eng: All the animals, all the livestock, all the birds, and everything crawling on the ground, came out of the ark by their families.
local: Ní tiwaŋkuti nì ipeel nì tin fù ti-biŋŋi puee nì tin kɔkɔ fù kitiŋ puee, ti-kɔkɔ nì ti-buluku ti-buluku nyáń meela poon.

eng: and named him Noah, saying, This one will give us relief from our hard work, from the pain in our hands, because of the fertile land that the LORD cursed.
local: U nìn pú-kì diyindi yii Noowa ní ki bí yii: «Un bí nee di làá cáań-ti dipɔɔsɔŋkil ti-Dindaan nín tɔ́ kitiŋ mbusu ti kɔ́ agìŋ́kpaan ŋin poonee.».

eng: The waters rose even higher over the earth; they covered all of the highest mountains under the sky.
local: Nnyim mɔ̄tìi gbí ki jɔ́m̀ ki tin bīiǹ ajoon ŋin fɔ̀kɔ̀ tikpilee kɔkɔ pu.

eng: God saw everything he had made: it was supremely good. There was evening and there was morning: the sixth day.
local: Unimbɔti ká yii tin kɔkɔ u ŋée ŋān̄ tikpil. Ní kunyeeu mɔ́ ní kutaa dūu wúntì, ŋwinluluuti dal dee.

eng: Lamech took two wives, the first named Adah and the second Zillah.
local: Ní Leemeeki cáá binimpoobi bili, bi yíì upeekaa yii Ada, ki yíì uliliiti yii Siila.

eng: From all living things—from all creatures—you are to bring a pair, male and female, into the ark with you to keep them alive.
local: Á dāá lēē tin kɔkɔ cáá kufoouee, kubuluu kamaa uja nì usal kí kɔ́ǹ meelan sii nì bi ń ŋmátí.

eng: Noah built an altar to the LORD. He took some of the clean large animals and some of the clean birds, and placed entirely burned offerings on the altar.
local: Noowa nyáńee, ní ki máń ti-Dindaan nsaraafan, ní ki lēē tiwaŋkuti tin kɔkɔ ŋān̄ bí dūú ŋá saraayee poon tiba, nì inoon in kɔkɔ ŋān̄ bí dūú ŋá saraayee poon iba ki dūu wùn̄ ŋŋmi nsaraafanee pu ki ŋá saraa ki tīī ti-Dindaan.

eng: After Noahs birth, Lamech lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Leemeeki nín màl̄ Noowa boonee, u ŋá abin aŋmoo kpá alaataa aluu. U nìn gíti màl̄ mbininjabiyaamu nì nnimpubiyaamu ki kútì.

eng: The waters rose twenty-three feet high, covering the mountains.
local: Nnyim gbí ki bīiǹ ajoon pu ki páaǹ kii meetab biluli pu.

eng: Adah gave birth to Jabal; he was the ancestor of those who live in tents and own livestock.
local: Ní Ada màl̄ Yabaal, úǹ di sá bin kɔkɔ kóò tantib poon ki yìiǹ ki kpàá inaayee naanja.

eng: From each kind of bird, from each kind of livestock, and from each kind of everything that crawls on the ground—a pair from each will go in with you to stay alive.
local: Á dāá lēē unoon kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kuwaŋkuluu kamaa buluku uja nì usal, nì kun kamaa pée fù kitiŋ puee buluku uja nì usal, bi-kɔkɔ ń dāá dāan̄ a-cee kí ŋmátí.

eng: The LORD smelled the pleasing scent, and the LORD thought to himself, I will not curse the fertile land anymore because of human beings since the ideas of the human mind are evil from their youth. I will never again destroy every living thing as I have done.
local: U-saraauee sɔnsɔfiŋu kɔ́ ti-Dindaan ki mɔ́ɔkiǹ-u ní u lī u-pɔbilin yii: «Kí yòon̄ díǹ kí nīn cée, maa làá ti bīil̀ kitiŋ unil pu. Báà nì min doooo unil biyatin ní u-pɔbil sá dikpitilee, maa làá ti dáaĺ tin kɔkɔ cáá kufoouee tafal kii m nín dáaĺ pu nee.

eng: In all, Lamech lived years, and he died.
local: Abin ŋin kɔkɔ Leemeeki ŋá ki nín kpíìyee kpáfì abin alaataa aluli nì abin ata kpá imuŋku inaa ní.

eng: Every creature took its last breath: the things crawling on the ground, birds, livestock, wild animals, everything swarming on the ground, and every human being.
local: Tin kɔkɔ nìn pée cáá kufoou ki bí kitiŋ puee, an dàkà yii inoon nì tiwaŋkuti nì ipeel nì tin kɔkɔ pée kpátì ki gbí kitiŋ puee, ki kpáaǹ binib, kɔkɔ kpíì múb.

eng: His brothers name was Jubal; he was the ancestor of those who play stringed and wind instruments.
local: U-naal ní bi nìn yíì Yubaal. Úǹ di sá bin bɔ̀ɔ́ tibati ki búǹ awulee naanja.

eng: Take some from every kind of food and stow it as food for you and for the animals.
local: Á dāá nyàab̀ tin kɔkɔ pée sá tijinee mun kí kpāafì án dāá nīn sá sii nì tin kɔkɔ bí a-ceeyee jinti.».

eng: As long as the earth exists, seedtime and harvest, cold and hot, summer and autumn, day and night will not cease.
local: Iyunti in kɔkɔ kitiŋ cáá kí nīn bée, tiwan dibɔldi nì tijin dical, tiwool nì kutuntuŋu, dipeel nì kuseeu, ŋwiimpu nì kunyeeu kpá ki gbèen̄.».

eng: When Noah was years old, Noah became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
local: Noowa nìn cáá abin alaataa aŋmoo ní ki màl̄ Seem nì Kaam nì Jaafeeti.

eng: Everything on dry land with lifes breath in its nostrils died.
local: Tin kɔkɔ nìn pée bí ki cáá kufoou ki fòotī ki bí kitiŋ puee kpíì.
